reasoning: We are forecasting whether the 10-year term premium over two short-term horizons (7 days and 30 days) will be higher than its starting value on Jan 18. Financial metrics like the term premium generally behave as a random walk over such short time horizons, implying roughly a 50% chance of an increase and a 50% chance of a decrease. No strong momentum or seasonal trend can systematically override this random walk baseline without explicit data indicating a trend. Without recent series data from the FRED fetch, the best estimate remains the mathematical baseline for a random walk with no predictable drift, which is 50%. Because both dates are relatively near-term, there is no reason to deviate from the maximum uncertainty baseline (50%).
